Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, suggesting possible chronic low glucose or recent dietary changes. Elevated creatinine may indicate reduced renal function. Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, proteinuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), raising concern for renal impairment and possible metabolic disturbance. Recommend clinical correlation for diabetes, renal function, and possible urinary tract pathology.
